meta_title: For Students: analytics setup to Solve stuck with basic apps (Intermediate Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to analytics setup and overcome stuck with basic apps. A Intermediate-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.
h2_structure: The Problem: stuck with basic apps

content.seo_title: For Students: Analytics Setup to Scale Your AI Projects
content.seo_description: Stop building apps in the dark. Learn how to implement professional analytics to validate your AI projects and level up your developer portfolio today.
content.answer_block: To move beyond basic apps, you must implement event-driven analytics. By integrating tracking into your core workflows, you transform guesswork into data-driven confidence. Using Antigravity for schema modeling and Claude Code for implementation, you can visualize user behavior, validate your features, and prove your project's real-world impact to future employers.
content.intro:
You’ve built the CRUD app, the simple AI wrapper, and the basic landing page. Yet, you feel stuck in a loop of 'tutorial hell,' unable to prove if anyone actually uses what you build. The silence of your analytics dashboard is a deafening reminder that code without user insight is just a digital ghost town.

Real-world development isn't about writing more lines of code; it's about understanding the user journey. By integrating professional-grade analytics, you bridge the gap between a student project and a production-ready SaaS. Let's turn your code into a measurable success story.

steps.title: Define Your North Star Metrics
steps.description: Before writing code, identify the key user actions that define success. We need to track 'Value Moments' rather than just page views.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior Product Engineer. Define a tracking schema for a SaaS AI app that captures user onboarding completion, feature usage frequency, and churn indicators. Provide a JSON schema for these events.
steps.claude_code_command: claude ask 'Generate a tracking documentation file named ANALYTICS.md based on my core features'

steps.title: Standardize the Event Schema
steps.description: Consistent data structures prevent 'dirty data' later. We create a centralized event emitter to handle all analytics calls.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a TypeScript analytics wrapper module that standardizes event names and payloads, ensuring type safety for all user interactions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create src/utils/analytics.ts with a type-safe event logger'

steps.title: Inject Tracking into AI Flows
steps.description: Capture the 'AI latency' and 'success rate' of your LLM calls to identify where users drop off.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Modify my existing AI service to track the duration of API calls and whether the user accepted or rejected the generated output.
steps.claude_code_command: claude edit src/services/ai.ts to add performance tracking hooks

steps.title: Automate User Context Enrichment
steps.description: Analytics are useless without context. Attach user metadata (plan type, signup date) to every event automatically.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a middleware that automatically enriches every analytics event with user session metadata and authentication status.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Implement an analytics middleware for our API routes'

steps.title: Validate and Debug Data Streams
steps.description: Use Claude Code to verify that events are firing correctly in your development environment before pushing to production.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Provide a testing script to mock user behavior and verify that all analytics events are being captured and formatted correctly.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a test suite in tests/analytics.test.ts to verify event dispatching'

pro_tips: Track the 'Time to First Value' (TTFV) metric; it is the single most important KPI for any student SaaS project.
Never send PII (Personally Identifiable Information) in your event payloads; use anonymized user IDs instead.
Use sampling for high-frequency events to keep your analytics costs low while maintaining statistical significance.
Set up automated alerts for when key conversion events drop by more than 20% over a 24-hour window.
Treat your analytics codebase as a product feature, not an afterthought; document it in your README for portfolio reviews.
content.next_steps: Deploy your analytics-enabled app to a staging environment, invite three friends to test it, and review the dashboard to see where they get stuck.
content.conversion_block: You are no longer just a student writing code; you are an engineer building products that matter. With this analytics foundation, you have the data to iterate, improve, and showcase a professional-grade portfolio piece. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
